notes: Features 1-15 were built and verified in the initial session (see claude-progress.md). 16+ are open work. Only flip passes->true after verified end-to-end testing.

features.description: MCP server exposing capture_start / capture_stop / capture_status over stdio
acceptance_criteria: mcp.list_tools() returns the three tools with correct schemas
capture_start requires exactly one of command/pid/app_name

features.description: Launch-mode process capture with timestamped stdout/stderr logging
acceptance_criteria: Launching a command writes stdout.log, stderr.log, and merged ISO-timestamped output.log
Process exit code is recorded

features.description: Window discovery via Quartz (pid / app-name -> CGWindowID)
acceptance_criteria: find_windows() returns layer-0 windows largest-first
primary_window(app_name=...) resolves a running app's main window

features.description: Periodic, grid-scheduled timestamped screenshots (window or whole-screen fallback)
acceptance_criteria: Screenshots saved as <iso-stamp>.<ext> at the configured interval
Window targeted via screencapture -l when resolvable, else whole-screen
rc=0-but-missing-file is treated as an error (screencapture quirk)

features.description: Configurable screenshot format, resolution (aspect-preserving), and JPEG quality
acceptance_criteria: screenshot_resolution accepts 'WxH' and 'WxH/fmt' (e.g. 1280x720/jpg)
Output fits within the box preserving aspect ratio, never upscaled
Format honored (png/jpg/...) via screencapture -t or sips re-encode

features.description: Audio pipeline: read PCM -> fixed windows -> ASR -> transcript.jsonl/txt with absolute timestamps
acceptance_criteria: 20s of audio chunks into 8+8+4 with monotonically advancing offsets
Timeline anchored to first-byte wall clock (not session t0)
raw audio saved to audio.s16le

features.description: Pluggable ASR with local Whisper default (mlx-whisper / faster-whisper)
acceptance_criteria: asr.create('auto'|'local') returns a working backend on arm64
Real speech transcribes correctly (e.g. 'the quick brown fox ...')
CAPTURE_WHISPER_MODEL env overrides the model

features.description: Session orchestrator ties process + screenshots + audio with session.json metadata and graceful degradation
acceptance_criteria: A failed component start rolls back others and re-raises
Audio failure (e.g. -3805) is surfaced in audio_status while screenshots/logs continue
session.json written on start, stop, and error

features.description: ScreenCaptureKit Swift helper streams per-app audio as 16kHz mono s16le
acceptance_criteria: swiftc builds helper/audiocap cleanly
Helper enumerates content, reaches startCapture, prints READY, and emits PCM bytes
--system whole-display mode works; clean exit on SIGTERM/SIGINT

features.description: Async MCP handlers offload blocking work via anyio.to_thread (no event-loop stalls)
acceptance_criteria: capture_start/stop/status are async and use anyio.to_thread.run_sync
anyio declared as a direct dependency

features.description: Bounded session registry with oldest-finished eviction
acceptance_criteria: _prune_locked keeps running sessions and evicts oldest finished beyond MAX_SESSIONS

features.description: Hermetic smoke test suite (tests/smoke.py)
acceptance_criteria: python tests/smoke.py prints 20/20 passed without special permissions

features.description: Remote NVIDIA Riva / Nemotron-3.5 ASR adapter (offline_recognize) with function-id/API-key auth
acceptance_criteria: Adapter constructs Auth/ASRService and maps RecognitionConfig + word offsets
VERIFIED against a real Riva endpoint returning timestamped transcripts

features.description: ffmpeg microphone fallback verified end-to-end
acceptance_criteria: audio_source='mic' captures real mic audio and produces a transcript
Graceful message when Microphone permission is missing

features.description: Stable code-signing identity so the helper's Screen Recording (TCC) grant persists across rebuilds. scripts/setup_codesign.sh creates the self-signed cert. Session 9: fixed two bugs that made it fail on macOS+OpenSSL-3 — PKCS#12 export now uses -legacy + a non-empty passphrase (empty/AES-MAC p12 fail `security import` MAC verification), and have_identity() detects via `find-identity -p codesigning` WITHOUT -v (a self-signed cert is untrusted so never lists under -v). The skill installer (skills/capture/scripts/install.sh) now runs setup_codesign.sh instead of an ad-hoc build, so skill-based installs get the persistent grant too.
acceptance_criteria: scripts/setup_codesign.sh creates a codesigning identity and signs the helper (DONE; fixed for OpenSSL 3)
build_helper.sh signs with CODESIGN_IDENTITY (DONE)
Attaching to a playing app yields non-empty transcript.jsonl, no fatal -3805 (DONE via auto-reconnect)
After setup_codesign, helper carries Authority=capture-mcp-codesign (not adhoc) and `audiocap --system` reports READY (DONE; verified on the macOS box Session 9)
skill install.sh stably signs the helper so skill installs also get a persistent grant (DONE)

features.description: True streaming ASR to avoid chunk-boundary word splits (Riva streaming_recognize; overlap+dedup or streaming-capable local backend)
acceptance_criteria: Words spanning window boundaries are not split or lost
Latency lower than the fixed-window approach

features.description: Optional: auto-stop a launch-mode session when the launched process exits
acceptance_criteria: When the child exits, screenshots/audio stop and state becomes 'stopped' without an explicit capture_stop

features.description: Package the helper as a signed .app bundle with NS* usage descriptions for distributable TCC
acceptance_criteria: Bundled helper requests permissions via its own Info.plist and is notarizable

features.description: CI / pytest harness running the hermetic suite
acceptance_criteria: pytest green locally; smoke test wrapped as pytest cases

features.description: Platform abstraction layer: interfaces (ScreenGrabber/WindowFinder/AudioSource) + a current() factory so OS-specific capture is swappable. macOS code moves behind it unchanged. Design in docs/specs/platform-abstraction.md.
acceptance_criteria: src/capture_mcp/platform/ with base interfaces + macos backend wrapping today's behavior
session/screenshots/audio/windows call platform.current() instead of importing macOS APIs directly
smoke test still 20/20 on macOS through the abstraction

features.description: Windows backends: screenshots (Windows.Graphics.Capture or PrintWindow), window discovery (EnumWindows), per-app audio (WASAPI process loopback) emitting the same 16kHz mono s16le contract.
acceptance_criteria: On Windows, capture_start produces timestamped screenshots of a target window
Per-app (or system) audio yields a transcript; mic fallback via ffmpeg dshow/wasapi
Session output layout + tool params identical to macOS

features.description: Windows bootstrap: init.ps1 equivalent of init.sh (venv, install with platform-gated extras, run smoke). Make pyobjc/mlx optional deps gated by platform.
acceptance_criteria: init.ps1 sets up a working env on a clean Windows box and runs tests/smoke.py green
pyproject extras gate macOS-only (pyobjc) and arm64-only (mlx) deps by platform

features.description: Benchmark local Whisper (faster-whisper CUDA) vs NVIDIA Nemotron-3.5 ASR (via the Riva adapter) on the Windows/NVIDIA box, on identical captured audio. Report WER, real-time factor/latency, GPU memory.
acceptance_criteria: Both backends transcribe the same audio.s16le clips via the ASRBackend interface
A results doc compares accuracy and speed; verifies the Riva/Nemotron adapter end-to-end (closes #13)

features.description: Redistributable 'capture' skill (skills/capture/): one-load install + .mcp.json wiring + quick capture actions for any project. Authored/validated with skill-creator.
acceptance_criteria: install.sh clones+installs capture-mcp (venv, ASR backend, helper) and prints the bin path (DONE)
configure_mcp.py creates/merges .mcp.json preserving other servers (DONE)
set_model.py changes + optionally prefetches the ASR model (DONE)
report_issue.py files a tracked GitHub bug with redacted diagnostics; preview-by-default, posts only with --create + user consent (DONE)
.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/bug_report.md provides a structured bug template (DONE)
skill passes skill-creator quick_validate and packages via package_skill.py (DONE)
install.ps1 installs capture-mcp on Windows (find Python, venv, faster-whisper extra, smoke) and prints the bin path; SKILL.md documents macOS+Windows install paths (DONE, Session 9)

features.description: M0a — engine/MCP package split + SessionRegistry: move engine modules to capture/core (verbatim), thin capture/mcp server; extract the _sessions dict/_prune_locked from server.py into a SessionRegistry that rebuilds history from <output_dir>/capture-*/session.json at startup; fix start() holding session._lock through slow component startup (add a 'starting' state, start components outside the lock, mirroring stop()). See docs/specs/product-architecture.md.
acceptance_criteria: MCP behavior identical: tools/list schemas unchanged, smoke 20/20 (DONE; suite grew to 28/28)
Server restart no longer loses session history (registry rebuilt from session.json files) (DONE; verified cross-process, Session 10)
capture-mcp console script still works; capture_status responds while another session is starting (DONE; smoke test_status_during_start)

features.description: M0b — EventBus + per-session events.jsonl: components push screenshot_taken/transcript_segment/log_line/state events alongside today's polled counters; CaptureSession appends an append-only events.jsonl per session (state transitions + periodic counter snapshots) so future GUI/daemon clients tail one file instead of fs-watching.
acceptance_criteria: A smoke capture produces events.jsonl with state transitions and counter snapshots (DONE; starting->running->stopping->stopped + periodic and final snapshots, smoke 35/35)
Existing polling (capture_status/summary()) unchanged (DONE; all pre-existing smoke checks pass untouched)

features.description: M0c — contract fixtures + frozen helper contract: tests/contract/ with a golden MCP tools/list schema dump, a recorded StubASR session-dir fixture (session.json + transcript.jsonl + events.jsonl layout), and PCM chunk/offset fixtures for the 8s-window logic; docs/specs/helper-contract.md freezing the audiocap protocol (argv, 16kHz mono s16le PCM on stdout, READY scanned on stderr, -3801/-3803 fatal vs -3805 transient); fix the audiocap.swift:14 'first line is READY' comment lie.
acceptance_criteria: Contract tests pass against current code and fail on schema/layout drift (DONE; 3/3 hold, injected drift exits 1, --regen path works)
helper-contract.md exists and matches audiocap.swift + audiocap_win.py reality (DONE; also fixed the READY comment lie, the audiocap_win shutdown NameError, and the phantom-watchdog docstring)

features.description: asr/openai_compat.py — small backend POSTing WAV-wrapped PCM to any OpenAI-compatible /v1/audio/transcriptions endpoint, plus a 'minimal' extra in pyproject.toml (screenshots+logs-only install). Turns the Nemotron WSL2/Docker lab into a plain remote endpoint.
acceptance_criteria: asr_backend pointing at a compatible endpoint yields timestamped transcript segments (DONE; smoke: direct backend + full AudioCapture pipeline against a stub server, 6 segments at offsets 0.5/2.0/8.5/10.0/16.5/18.0)
pip install 'capture-mcp[minimal]' captures screenshots+logs with no ASR deps (DONE; verified in a fresh uv venv: no mlx/faster-whisper/riva importable, real capture produced screenshots+logs)

features.description: list_windows MCP tool — expose WindowFinder discovery (title, app, pid, window id, bounds) so agents get the same window picker the GUI will use.
acceptance_criteria: tools/list shows list_windows; returns layer-0 windows largest-first on macOS and EnumWindows results on Windows (DONE on macOS: 7 real windows largest-first, app_name filter verified, golden updated; Windows backend goes through the same WindowFinder.find — verify on the Windows box next time it runs the suite)

features.description: TCC attribution spike (GATES the daemon architecture): on a clean macOS 14 and 15 VM, verify that a launchd-agent-spawned, Developer-ID-signed PyInstaller daemon is the TCC-responsible process for Screen Recording, that the grant persists across app updates (same Team ID + bundle id), the daemon-restart-after-grant flow, and the macOS 15 periodic re-approval behavior. Document as a repeatable checklist in docs/specs/product-architecture.md.
acceptance_criteria: Written spike report: attribution works (or the documented fallback decision if it does not) (DONE 2026-06-15, macOS 26.5.1/arm64: PASS — launchd→signed .app PyInstaller daemon→audiocap streamed audio with no terminal grant; spike/tcc-attribution/results/FINDINGS.md)

Grant survives a simulated app update with unchanged identity/bundle id (DONE: same-identity rebuild+re-sign → audio flowed immediately, respawns=0, no re-prompt; identity ROTATION lost the grant — negative control confirms keying to the signing identity)

re-approval / robustness notes recorded (DONE: macOS 26 SCShareableContent enumeration is intermittently flaky — audiocap exit 5 rode by the respawn loop; follow-up logged to add a bounded enumeration retry to audiocap.swift)

features.description: M1 — packaged signed engine, no GUI: PyInstaller onedir of the engine+MCP, Developer ID + notarization, PREBUILT signed audiocap (kills the swiftc/Xcode-CLT requirement for end users), capture doctor (permission preflight, CGRequestScreenCaptureAccess trigger, System Settings deep link), distributed via brew tap + GitHub releases.
acceptance_criteria: brew install <tap>/capture then 'grant once' yields a working audio capture on a machine with no git/Xcode CLT/Python
Packaged-bundle smoke: a real capture runs from the installed artifact
From-source init.sh path still works unchanged

features.description: M2 — captured daemon + /v1 API + CLI: aiohttp HTTP+WS over UDS+bearer token (127.0.0.1 on Windows), endpoint discovery via ~/.capture/daemon.json (0600), /v1 surface per docs/specs/product-architecture.md, daemon-level ASR model pool, capture CLI (start/stop/status/tail/doctor/daemon install|uninstall) writing launchd agent / logon task / systemd user unit; MCP server gains daemon-first mode with embedded fallback (CAPTURE_MCP_EMBEDDED=1).
acceptance_criteria: Agent-started capture is visible via CLI status; sessions survive client exits (DONE: captured /v1 API on 127.0.0.1+token over a shared SessionRegistry; `capture` CLI + the MCP server (daemon-first) are both clients; smoke test_daemon + test_cli_daemon + test_mcp_daemon_first, 62/62)

One Screen Recording grant (to the daemon) gives working audio from two different terminals' MCP agents (MECHANISM DONE: both terminals' MCP agents proxy to the one daemon via daemon-first dispatch, so they share its registry+grant. The stable-grant BENEFIT still needs the packaged signed launchd daemon #31/#30 to be the TCC-responsible process)

JSON-Schema round-trip contract tests pass; pydantic schema checked in (DONE: daemon/models.py pydantic models = the /v1 contract; v1_schema() + GET /v1/schema; golden tests/contract/golden/v1_schema.json + smoke round-trips live responses through the models + 400 on bad request; registry now returns uniformly full-shaped records so SessionSummary holds. Rust typify generation still planned.)

[DONE] daemon: stdlib /v1 (health/sessions/windows/transcript/admin-shutdown), bearer-token auth (401 without), single-instance guard, ~/.capture/daemon.json (0600); `captured`+`capture` scripts. MCP server: daemon-first dispatch with embedded fallback (CAPTURE_MCP_EMBEDDED=1). docs/specs/daemon.md + mcp-server.md.

[DONE] live event stream GET /v1/events (SSE) fanning each session's EventBus out, tagged with session_id, + `capture watch` CLI; smoke test_sse_events (full lifecycle + log_line/screenshot pushed live); verified with `capture watch` on a real run.

[REMAINING for passes:true] UDS transport (SSE replaces the planned WS for events); daemon lifecycle install (launchd/systemd/logon-task); Rust type generation from /v1 schema; the cross-terminal-AUDIO benefit (needs #31)

features.description: M3 — GPUI app v1 on macOS: tray/menu-bar icon (tray-icon+muda), main window (session list from daemon registry, new-capture form with /v1/windows picker), session detail (live preview via RenderImage with cache eviction, transcript/log tails), onboarding (SMAppService daemon registration, Grant flow with permission.updated push, ends with a visible 5-second self-test capture), Settings (ASR model manager with resumable+SHA256 downloads). GPUI pinned to a frozen zed git rev + compatible gpui-component rev, cargo vendor, quarterly bump policy.
acceptance_criteria: Capture.dmg: drag-install, onboarding to first successful self-test capture without a terminal (PENDING: packaging is M4-adjacent, needs Developer ID #31)

An MCP-agent-started capture appears live in the GUI session list (DONE slice 1: gui/ GPUI app polls /v1/sessions; verified — captures started via the CLI/GUI appear in the GUI list with live shots/segs counts, and history sessions recovered from the disk index show too)
Quitting the GUI does not stop a running capture (DONE: the GUI is a thin daemon client; the daemon owns the session — verified, captures outlive the GUI process)

[DONE slices 1-2, macOS, gpui 0.2.2] gui/: daemon client (health/sessions/windows/start/stop/transcript + /v1/events SSE reader) + GPUI window — health header, /v1/windows picker, Start/Stop, live-polled session list, and a LIVE session-detail pane (screenshot preview via img() + transcript streamed over SSE; auto-opens the running capture). Built+ran+screenshotted: transcript grew live and the preview rendered the video frame. docs/specs/gui.md.

[DONE slice 3] menu-bar (tray) presence via tray-icon 0.24 + muda 0.19 (gui/src/tray.rs): title reflects the running-capture count (● capture / ⦿ N, live-updated from the GPUI poll), Open/Stop-all/Quit menu drained on the main-thread tray loop. Verified: title went ● capture -> ⦿ 1 -> ● capture across a CLI start/stop.

[DONE slice 4] global hotkey ⌃⌘R (global-hotkey 0.8, Carbon — no accessibility prompt) toggles capture from anywhere (running -> stop all; else start on selected window), drained in the tray loop. Registration verified (GUI shows the '⌃⌘R toggles capture' hint = manager+register succeeded); the live key-press path needs a real hardware press (synthetic keystrokes need Accessibility / don't trigger Carbon hotkeys).

[DONE slice 5] GUI installs/updates the bundled `capture` skill into a coding agent's home (~/.claude/skills/capture, ~/.codex/skills/capture) with a content-hash status check (— install / ✓ / ↑ update); verified via --skill-status/--install-skill headless flags (not installed→up to date→update available→up to date).

[DONE slice 6] packaging/build_macos_dmg.sh builds Capture.app (ad-hoc signed, bundles the skill) + Capture-<v>.dmg for macOS testing; README documents the Gatekeeper bypass (right-click Open / Open Anyway / xattr -dr quarantine). NOT notarized — Developer ID is #31.

[DONE slice 7] self-contained bundle: build_macos_dmg.sh now PyInstaller-freezes the daemon into Capture.app/Contents/Resources/captured/ (with the signed audiocap helper beside it, keeping its stable com.local.audiocap identity), and the GUI auto-spawns it (daemon.rs bundled_daemon()/spawn_detached(), called from app.rs new(); poll+SSE re-discover so they attach to the just-spawned daemon). No separate venv / `capture daemon start`. Verified: the frozen daemon copied OUT of the .app boots, /v1/health ok, /v1/windows returns windows (Quartz works frozen); app signature verifies --strict; DMG is 28M. Transcription still needs a configured ASR backend (mlx excluded to keep the bundle small).

[REMAINING for passes:true] RenderImage with eviction for the preview (img() cache leak on long runs); a menu-bar icon (vs text title) + LSUIElement; onboarding + Settings/ASR-model manager; Developer ID signing + notarization (#31); migrate gpui 0.2.2 -> pinned zed git rev for Linux/a11y

features.description: M4 — Windows release: GPUI app on the DX11 backend, Inno Setup installer (+ winget), logon-task daemon install, interactive-station preflight UX, and per-process audio via a NATIVE helper binary (windows-rs, ActivateAudioInterfaceAsync + PROCESS_LOOPBACK with INCLUDE_TARGET_PROCESS_TREE for Chromium-family apps) speaking the frozen 16kHz s16le PCM stdout contract — closes the per-process half of #21. Ship with honest SmartScreen messaging (Trusted Signing unavailable to individuals; OV cert does not clear cold-start).
acceptance_criteria: CaptureSetup-x64.exe installs and first capture works without a terminal
Per-process loopback isolates a Chromium app's audio while other apps play sound
GUI labels system-mix vs per-process source correctly

features.description: M5 — Linux: third platform backend (X11 window discovery + screenshots first; Wayland via xdg-desktop-portal ScreenCast — picker-only targeting, persistent share indicator, re-persisted restore tokens, documented app_name degradation; PipeWire per-app audio with a reconnect-tracking helper using -3805-style semantics), systemd user unit, then GPUI app on wgpu (Vulkan requirement documented; engine artifact must not link GPUI so headless/SSH agents keep working).
acceptance_criteria: MCP agent captures screenshots+logs on X11 Linux with no GUI installed
Wayland capture works through the portal flow with documented semantics
PipeWire per-app audio yields a transcript for a target app while another app plays sound
